articles.title: Zimbabwe shifts to beneficiation, targets strong ferrochrome industry

articles.title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
articles.description: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Bayern late show sinks Real Madrid 6-4 in epic quarter-final
more.description: MUNICH, Germany,- Bayern Munich’s Luis Diaz ​and Michael Olise scored late goals to earn the hosts a dramatic 4-3 win over Real ‌Madrid in the Champions League quarter-finals on Wednesday and send them into the last four 6-4 on aggregate after a thrilling tie. Diaz struck in the 89th minute and Olise in stoppage time […]The post Bayern late show sinks Real Madrid 6-4 in epic quarter-final appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Healthcare shake-up threatens 10 000 jobs
more.description: At least 10,000 jobs could be lost if government proceeds with plans to bar medical aid societies from owning clinics and hospitals, stakeholders in Zimbabwe’s healthcare sector have warned. The concerns arise from proposed amendments by the Ministry of Health and Child Care to the Medical Aid Societies Regulations, governed under Statutory Instrument 330 of […]The post Healthcare shake-up threatens 10 000 jobs appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Zimbabwe’s Re-Engagement Drive Gains Traction as UK and Germany Signal Support for Debt Clearance Strategy
more.description: HARARE – Zimbabwe has secured commitments from the United Kingdom and Germany to support its economic reform agenda, as authorities intensify efforts to resolve external debt arrears and re-engage international financial institutions, according to the Ministry of Finance. The Treasury, in an update posted on its official X (formerly Twitter) account on April 14, said […]The post Zimbabwe’s Re-Engagement Drive Gains Traction as UK and Germany Signal Support for Debt Clearance Strategy appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Chamisa trashes ‘fake news’ claims over CAB3
more.description: THE beleagaured former Citizens Coalition for Change (CCC) leader Nelson Chamisa has pushed back against allegations that he has “sold out the struggle for democracy”, describing the claims as part of a “propaganda campaign of fake news”. The accusations from some opposition activists stem from Chamisa’s perceived lack of strong public engagement against the controversial […]The post Chamisa trashes ‘fake news’ claims over CAB3 appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: Finance minister Ncube orders suspension of duty on public service buses
more.description: HARARE – The government has suspended customs duty on public service bus imports, backdated to January 1 this year, in a move aimed at easing commuter transport challenges and modernising the country’s public transit fleet. Finance minister Mthuli Ncube said the move would “lower commuting costs, and modernise the fleet by reducing import costs for […]The post Finance minister Ncube orders suspension of duty on public service buses appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail.

more.title: 'Friend to All' - Zimbabwe Hails Foreign Policy Gains Since 2017
more.description: [263Chat] The government says its foreign policy of being a "friend to all and an enemy to none" has delivered major economic and diplomatic gains since 2017.

more.title: Ex-Health Registrar Wins Bail As He Appeals Abuse of Office Conviction
more.description: [263Chat] The High Court has granted US$500 bail to Collin Benyure, the former Registrar of the Medical and Dental Practitioners Council of Zimbabwe who was serving a 12-month jail term for criminal abuse of duty.

more.title: Farmers urged to improve grading as tobacco quality shapes early-season prices
more.description: FARMERS have been urged to prioritise proper grading, baling and presentation of their crop as prices begin to firm at both the contract and auction floors. The 2026 tobacco marketing season has opened with a strong presence of lower leaf positions, commonly known as primings, which are naturally priced lower than upper leaf grades. This [...] The post Farmers urged to improve grading as tobacco quality shapes early-season prices appeared first on The Zimbabwe Mail .

more.title: Sanctions, Systems and Sovereignty: How China Is Rewiring Global Power in a Fragmenting Dollar Order
more.description: When Russia launched its “special military operation” in Ukraine in 2022, global attention largely centred on battlefield dynamics. Yet in China, the focus shifted to a far more consequential domain: the architecture of sanctions and the financial plumbing of global power. more.title: Practical Tips for Spotting and Avoiding Momentum Traps in Live Sports Events
more.description: Reading Through Momentum Traps in Real Time The scoreboard moves, the crowd noise spikes through your screen, and a team rattles off three unanswered goals in twelve minutes. Everything about that sequence screams opportunity. That instinct to jump in right there, mid-surge, is exactly what separates profitable live bettors from everyone feeding the bookmaker’s margin.
